title: Fluorescent staining of cells on 3D broccoli-based scaffolds
author: Catherine A. Sword
materials: Aspirate the solution from the scaffolds.

steps.title: Seed fibroblasts at the desired density onto 3
steps.instruction: Seed fibroblasts at the desired density onto 3.5 mm scaffolds as described in the "Seeding and culturing adherent cells on 3D broccoli-based scaffolds" protocol except include 50 μg/ml human plasma fibronectin Corning Catalog #354008 in the final culture medium. Seeding and culturing adherent cells on 3D broccoli-based scaffolds by Kristopher E Kubow The proof-of-concept experiments in the associated manuscript used mouse embyronic fibroblasts (MEFs) seeded at densities between 1,000 and 15,000 cells per 3.5 mm scaffold. To seed 1,000 cells per scaffold, dilute the trypsinized cells to 10,000 cells/ml and prepare a 100 μg/ml fibronectin solution in growth medium. Add 100 μl each of the cell suspension and fibronectin solution to each scaffold. This results in 1,000 cells seeded with 50 μg/ml fibronectin in the medium. Alternatively, seed the cells at the desired density, wait until the cells are attached, and then exchange the medium for medium containing 50 μg/ml fibronectin.

steps.title: Culture for three days
steps.instruction: Culture for three days. Shorter culture times will result in less fibronectin ECM produced.

steps.title: Aspirate the medium from and add...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the medium from and add 200 µL sterile PBS to each scaffold. For this and all aspiration steps, avoid touching the scaffold with the aspirator tip so as to prevent damage to the scaffold. For 6.5 mm scaffolds, which completely occupy the bottom of the well (in a 96-well plate), tilt the well plate and aspirate down to the top surface of the scaffold but do not attempt to push the aspirator tip to the bottom of the well.

steps.title: Add 200 µL 4% formaldehyde in...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL 4% formaldehyde in PBS to each scaffold and incubate at Room temperature for 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Prepare 4 % volume formaldehyde (e
steps.instruction: Prepare 4 % volume formaldehyde (e.g. from 16% formaldehyde Electron Microscopy Sciences Catalog #100503-914 (CAS: 50-00-0) ) in Dulbeccos Phosphate-Buffered Saline no clacium no magnesium (DPBS-/-) Gibco - Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #14190-144 .

steps.title: Rinse the scaffolds three times with PBS:
steps.instruction: Rinse the scaffolds three times with PBS:

steps.title: Aspirate as much liquid as possible...
steps.instruction: Aspirate as much liquid as possible from the scaffolds and add 200 µL primary antibody staining solution to each scaffold.

steps.title: Prepare the primary staining solution
steps.instruction: Prepare the primary staining solution: 2 % volume BSA 1:300 Rabbit-anti-human fibronectin polyclonal antibody Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #F3648 in Dulbeccos Phosphate-Buffered Saline no clacium no magnesium (DPBS-/-) Gibco - Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #14190-144

steps.title: Add 200 µL PBS to each scaffold
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL PBS to each scaffold.

steps.title: Rinse the scaffolds with 200 µL PBS
steps.instruction: Rinse the scaffolds with 200 µL PBS.

steps.title: Incubate samples with 200 µL blocking/permeabilization...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples with 200 µL blocking/permeabilization solution for 01:00:00 at Room temperature or Overnight at 4 °C .

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00

steps.title: Prepare the blocking/permeabilization solution
steps.instruction: Prepare the blocking/permeabilization solution: dissolve 3 Mass / % volume Bovine Serum Albumin (BSA) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #A7030-100G 1 Mass / % volume Gelatin from cold water fish skin, 100g Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #G7041-100G 0.02 Mass / % volume Saponin Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #G7041-100G in Dulbeccos Phosphate-Buffered Saline no clacium no magnesium (DPBS-/-) Gibco - Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #14190-144 .

steps.title: Incubate samples for 01:00:00 at Room...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples for 01:00:00 at Room temperature or Overnight at 4 °C .

steps.title: Prepare the secondary staining solution
steps.instruction: Prepare the secondary staining solution: 2 % volume BSA 1:500 Donkey anti-Rabbit secondary antibody, Alexa Fluor 546 Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #A10040 5 μg/ml DAPI Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #D8417 1:400 Acti-Stain 488 Phalloidin Cytoskeleton Inc. Catalog #PHDG1 in PBS.

steps.title: Rinse scaffolds three times with PBS...
steps.instruction: Rinse scaffolds three times with PBS as in Step 7 ( go to step #7 )

steps.title: Aspirate as much liquid as possible...
steps.instruction: Aspirate as much liquid as possible from the scaffolds and add 200 µL secondary staining solution to each scaffold.

steps.title: Incubate samples for 01:00:00 at Room...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples for 01:00:00 at Room temperature or Overnight at 4 °C .

steps.title: Rinse scaffolds three times with PBS...
steps.instruction: Rinse scaffolds three times with PBS as in Step 7 ( go to step #7 )

steps.title: Use forceps to invert and transfer...
steps.instruction: Use forceps to invert and transfer scaffolds to a cover-glass bottomed dish or chamber (e.g. Ibidi 80807). Add enough PBS to the scaffolds to keep them hydrated, but not so much that the scaffolds float above the dish/well surface. Having the scaffolds stationary on the surface will make them easier to image. Because of the short incubation times, cells will be be located on the exterior surfaces of the scaffold, primarily on the side facing up during seeding. It is therefore recommended to invert the scaffolds for imaging using an inverted microscope.

steps.title: Image immediately using an inverted confocal microscope
steps.instruction: Image immediately using an inverted confocal microscope. For the proof-of-concept experiments in the associated manuscript, we used a Leica Stellaris 8 laser scanning confocal microscope with HC PL APO CS2 20x/0.75 and HC PL APO CS2 40x/1.25 glycerol-immersion objectives. DAPI was excited by a 405 diode laser and emission from 425-505 nm was collected on a HyD S detector. Acti-stain 488 Phalloidin was excited at 498 nm by a white light laser and emission from 505-547 nm was collected on a HyD X detector. The secondary antibody for fibronectin detection (donkey-anti-rabbit Alexa Fluor 546) was excited at 556 nm by a white light laser and emission from 561-814 nm was collected on a HyD S detector. The DAPI and Acti-stain 488 channels were acquired sequentially to avoid bleed-through.
